utility_evaluation.REASONING: There are 6 items in the required format: property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, setup_services, total_budget. In the final solution, the top-level keys are selected_property, financing, legal_services, moving_services, total_budget. Mapping them: property is present but named selected_property; financing is present; legal_services is present; moving_services is present; setup_services is not present as a top-level item (it's nested under moving_services instead of being its own item); total_budget is present. Next, check if each is fully specified: 1) property: includes type/name, area, bedrooms, price, and description, but the address is pending; still reasonably detailed, likely acceptable. 2) financing: includes options, terms like LTV, fees, and a recommended option; acceptable. 3) legal_services: includes legal package and survey with costs and details; acceptable. 4) moving_services: includes moving option with cost and details; acceptable. 5) setup_services: required as a separate top-level item but is included incorrectly under moving_services; therefore, as a separate item it is missing. 6) total_budget: includes property price, additional costs breakdown, and total additional costs; acceptable though no grand total including property, but still a breakdown is provided. Therefore, 5 of 6 items are included as top-level items exactly as requested; the setup_services item is not at the correct level.

llm_ratings_evaluation.REASONING: We identify items present in the final property solution and map them to the exact names in the ground-truth ratings. The solution includes: Property: Historic Townhouse; Financing options: Traditional Mortgage and First-Time Buyer Mortgage (called First-Time Buyer in the ratings); Legal package: Basic Legal Package; Property survey: Basic Survey; Moving: Standard Moving; Utilities setup: Basic Utilities Package (called Basic Utilities in the ratings). We then pull the corresponding ratings.
